keyRules: A maximum of 120 m above ground level (EASA Open category under a bilateral agreement)
Register as a UAS operator (CHF 10, valid indefinitely) if the drone weighs 250 g or more or is equipped with a camera
A1/A3 online training required for weights of 250 g or more (certificate valid for 5 years) — Minimum age 12; children under 12 must be supervised by someone 16 or older.
Mandatory liability insurance for drones weighing 250 g or more (coverage of CHF 1 million)
Members of model aircraft clubs: additional privileges under FOCA regulations
Always maintain visual line of sight (VLOS) with the drone
Do not fly over assemblies of people. Rules for uninvolved persons depend on subcategory, class mark and operating conditions; check the official matrix.
Within 5 km of airports and airfields, operation is permitted only with the airfield's authorization (drones weighing more than 250 g).
National Parks: Drone Use Restricted or Prohibited
Mountain regions: Be aware of helicopter flight paths (rescue, sightseeing flights)
importantNotes: Registration costs only CHF 10—a one-time fee with unlimited validity and no renewal required.
Mandatory Insurance for Drones Weighing 250 g or More (Coverage: CHF 1 Million)
Switzerland is not a member of the EU, but complies with EASA regulations through a bilateral agreement
Alpine helicopter routes are common—always keep an eye on the airspace
The DSG (Swiss Data Protection Act) applies when photographing identifiable individuals

fields.disclaimerText: Before every flight, always check the official sources from FOCA/BAZL and the official drone map at map.geo.admin.ch. This app is a tool—not a legal source. Switzerland follows EASA regulations through a bilateral agreement.
